namingDrift.note: The runner/sidecar component has two names across doc generations, both referring to the SAME component. Current docs and the current code say 'runner' (package at services/runner/, compose service 'runner', port :8765). Older docs (and some cross-references inside currently-live docs, e.g. architecture.md's own ASCII diagram and tools.md's 'Where this lives' table) still say 'sandbox-agent' and cite paths under services/agent/. services/agent/ does not exist in this checkout; only services/runner/ does. This model uses 'runner' as the canonical id/label everywhere and records 'sandbox-agent' and 'services/agent/*' as aliases/legacy paths on the runner node, per ground-truth.md's own framing of this exact drift ('Runner wire' row: 'services/agent/src/protocol.ts' listed as the code surface, which the checkout resolves to services/runner/src/protocol.ts).
namingDrift.resolution: Trusted the actual file tree (services/runner/src/*) over stale path citations embedded in otherwise-current docs, consistent with 'if a page disagrees with the code it cites, the code wins' (ground-truth.md's own stated policy).

resolvedAmbiguities.docClaim: tools.md describes it as forcing 'the effective policy to deny everywhere.'
resolvedAmbiguities.codeFinding: permission-plan.ts's permissionsFromRequest only forces the PLAN to {default: "deny", rules: []} before the wire permissions block is read. effectivePermission() still checks gate.specPermission and gate.serverPermission BEFORE consulting the plan default, so an explicit per-tool 'allow' still runs even with the kill-switch on.
resolvedAmbiguities.resolution: Modeled the code's narrower behavior in permissions.json (killSwitch.scopeNuance, testVectors TV13/TV14), since the task instructed reading permission-plan.ts directly for this logic. This is a precision gap in the prose doc, not a contradiction serious enough to file as a doc bug on its own, but worth flagging.

resolvedAmbiguities.topic: Which gate enforces on Claude for gateway/code tools
resolvedAmbiguities.docClaim: Multiple pages (tools.md, claude-code.md, permission-responder.md) describe this consistently once cross-read, but it takes three docs plus the relay code to see that RelayPermissions.enforce is literally false for Claude.
resolvedAmbiguities.codeFinding: relay.ts's executeRelayedTool only calls permissions.decide(...) when permissions.enforce is true. Confirmed both gates share one decide() so they cannot disagree, per permission-plan.ts.
resolvedAmbiguities.resolution: Modeled as three explicit gates in permissions.json (settings-file, acp-responder, relay) rather than collapsing Claude's two-layer gate into one, to make the enforcement matrix explicit for the explorer.

resolvedAmbiguities.docClaim: No design doc gives measured latency numbers for any stage of the spine.
resolvedAmbiguities.codeFinding: The only real numeric constants found anywhere in the runner are the tool-relay poll intervals (services/runner/src/tools/relay.ts: RELAY_POLL_MS=300, RELAY_POLL_MAX_MS=1500, RELAY_POLL_IDLE_GROW_AFTER=5, RELAY_TIMEOUT_MS=60000).
resolvedAmbiguities.resolution: loadmodel.json marks every other stage's illustrativeLatencyMs as empirical:false with a top-level disclaimer, and marks only the relay poll interval as empirical:true, per the task's explicit 'NO invented numbers' instruction.

gaps.summary: LocalBackend is a public SDK adapter shape; create_sandbox/create_session raise NotImplementedError. Never selected in the deployed stack.
No durable session store, no write path, no history-load endpoint. The runtime is cold; clients must resend full history every turn.
AgentaHarness (pi_agenta) ships placeholder preamble, persona, and skill content, though it does run end to end on sandbox-agent local and Daytona.
Per-request model override is not honored on the Pi ACP path; pi-acp accepts only its default model and silently falls back.
User-declared mcp_servers resolution is gated off by AGENTA_AGENT_ENABLE_MCP (default off); even when on, the runner drops user MCP for Pi, so it would reach Claude only.
HarnessCapabilities are probed per run and returned on the /run result, but consumed only for the internal mcpTools delivery branch; no /inspect surface, frontend gate, or service check reads them.
Trigger lifecycle, Compose.io integration, and event-to-agent mapping are not implemented; only the read-only discover_triggers platform op exists.
The filesystem boundary (on/readonly/off) travels on the wire and is versioned with the config, but no provider applies it anywhere.

Network egress enforcement is a hard boundary only on Daytona; the local sidecar runs on the runner host with no egress control, so a restricted policy on local is rejected outright under enforcement:strict.

Handler-mode platform ops (e.g. test_run) exist server-side behind AGENTA_AGENT_ENABLE_PLATFORM_HANDLERS (default off); the runner does not yet dispatch reserved call_refs with spec-level context injection or timeoutMs.
Code tools run with only the interpreter and their own declared secrets; no package install step and no module path to the runner's dependencies.
